summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between editing and viewing Claude Code sessions.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail view, native checkpoint tree sidebar, MCP server embedded at /mcp, search via QuickPick with FTS, image rendering, agent-aware labels and commands, VSIX packaging script, onboarding flow, and checkpoint remote support.

repo: Each repo gets its own SQLite DB at ~/.gossamer/repos/<name>.db; config at ~/.gossamer/config.json
The gossamer server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (worktree), NOT gossamer/dist/serve.js — changes to main repo must be synced and rebuilt in the worktree
Entire.io writes session data to entire/checkpoints/v1 branch and shadow branches; gossamer reads and indexes these into SQLite
Custom session titles from /rename are stored as custom-title events in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/*.jsonl, NOT in the checkpoint/shadow branches
Session slugs (auto-generated) are on LogEvent.slug; custom titles require reading Claude's local JSONL files directly
The .gossamer/checkpoints/ worktree directory is created inside each repo by ensureWorktree() — should be added to .gitignore
Entire.io only PUSHES to the checkpoint remote — it never fetches; Gossamer must fetch before indexing to get commits from other machines
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps checkpointId to gitOid; session cwd must be looked up to find the correct git repo for git show operations
better-sqlite3 native module compiled against different Node.js version than VS Code's bundled Node; server must be spawned as child process using system node
The schema.sql approach (prisma migrate diff --from-empty) allows schema application without the Prisma CLI in bundled VSIX; all statements need IF NOT EXISTS

code.finding: Server is a static singleton that survives panel disposal; getConfiguredPort() reads ~/.gossamer/config.json; SERVE_SCRIPT uses existsSync fallback for bundled vs dev mode
onDidChangeViewState listener calls checkpointProvider.setSession() when panel becomes active — required for sidebar to update on tab switch
Must use include: ["src/**/*.ts"] not individual files — missing files were never compiled causing silent runtime failures
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once; stored as window.__vscodeApi and reused to prevent duplicate call errors
root and outDir must be absolute paths using resolve(__dirname, ...) — relative paths resolve to CWD when run via workspace -w flag
schema.sql parsing bug: splitting on ';' leaves '-- CreateTable' comment prefixes which were incorrectly filtered; must strip comments from each chunk before executing

indexAllCheckpointsV2 reads checkpoint files via readdirSync/readFileSync on a worktree; the worktree working tree does NOT auto-update when Entire commits via plumbing — must git reset --hard before reading

workflow: VS Code webview panels reload completely when dragged to a new window; must re-send server_ready via onDidChangeViewState
VS Code's TreeView resourceUri only applies SCM decorations with absolute file paths — relative paths silently produce no decoration
esbuild can bundle the server but native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty, bindings, file-uri-to-path, node-addon-api) must be externalized and copied manually
VS Code webview CSP must include img-src data: https: http: for base64 image rendering
Vite ESM output requires type="module" on the script tag in the webview HTML — missing this causes silent no-op
Git worktrees cannot checkout the same branch in two places; create a new branch for the testing worktree

friction: gossamer-testing worktree has independent file copies — every change requires manual sync (cp) + rebuild, causing repeated 'still seeing old behavior' debugging loops
Server process survival across panel disposal made restart non-obvious; had to add explicit gossamer.restartServer command
Prisma CLI (62MB) cannot be bundled in VSIX; required generating schema.sql at build time as workaround
schema.sql comment stripping bug caused fresh install failure after the fix was supposedly applied
acquireVsCodeApi() called-once constraint caused blank panel when minimize button inline script and React app both tried to call it
tsconfig.extension.json only listing specific files meant new files (SessionDetailPanel.ts, diffUtils.ts) were silently never compiled
Server NODE_MODULE_VERSION mismatch (141 vs 140) required spawning via system node instead of VS Code's bundled node
Content provider approach for diffs was abandoned due to URI encoding fragility — switched to temp files then back to in-memory provider
Tab title showed slug instead of customTitle after server rebuild because webview JS in gossamer-testing was stale
Accidentally made Electron app changes when user wanted VS Code extension changes
open_items: Session color (set by user in Claude Code UI) is not stored anywhere accessible — cannot be surfaced in Gossamer
Session name from /rename only detected via ~/.claude/projects JSONL scanning — won't work for sessions on other machines where those files don't exist
ALTER TABLE migrations not handled — schema.sql IF NOT EXISTS approach won't add new columns to existing DBs
gossamer-testing worktree sync is manual — a script or git hook would reduce friction significantly
Multi-repo support in sessions list: sessions from different repos are shown in a flat list with cwd as the differentiator
Shadow branch indexing not triggered by checkpoint git watcher for all repos — only the configured repoDir triggers the watcher
Codex and Gemini CLI resume flags (--session vs --resume) are hardcoded guesses; need verification from actual CLI docs
Onboarding checkpoint remote step: the 'skip' flag resets on panel reopen — user could be repeatedly prompted
gossamer.vsix should be in .gitignore — was accidentally committed
MCP server HTTP transport is stateless — no session persistence between tool calls
